From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Estonian athletics competitor

**Liina Tšernov** (born 28 December 1987) is an [Estonian](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") athletics competitor.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

She was born in [Saku](/wiki/Saku,_Estonia "Saku, Estonia"), Harju County. She studied at the [University of Tartu](/wiki/University_of_Tartu "University of Tartu")'s Institute of Physical Education.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

She began her athletics career in 2010, coached by Tiiu Zirnask.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1) Later her coaches were Valter Espe and Endel Pärna.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1) She won bronze medal at [2014 European Athletics Team Championships](/wiki/2014_European_Athletics_Team_Championships "2014 European Athletics Team Championships") in 800 m and 1500 m.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] She is multiple-times Estonian champion in different running disciplines. 2015–2019 she was a member of Estonian national athletics team.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

Personal best:[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

* 800 m: 2.03,96 (2016)
* 3000 m: 9.01,45 (2017)
* 5000 m: 16.19,32 (2019)
